description: Simmons and Daisy work to uncover the identity of the Inhuman reported by the Senator. Daisy points out Vijay’s online activity may be fake, leading Simmons to suspect a cover-up. They discuss Fitz’s behavior and Simmons’s concerns over the Director’s methods. Their camaraderie and worries about the robot plotline intertwine, setting up their next mission.

transcript.line: Are you sure this is the Inhuman you saw in the husk?
Absolutely positive. I knew Senator Nadeer was connected to him somehow, and in my research on her, I recognized him, Vijay Nadeer, her own brother, if you can believe it.
Yeah, but there's zero evidence that he's been missing. He's been posting on Facebook, Twitter. His bills are paid. He even recently signed up for a Master Class in directing.
But there have been no new photos of Vijay in the last seven months.
So either he's suddenly gotten camera-shy or...
Not someone, the Senator, considering she's the country's leading anti-Inhuman hatemonger.
I'd have a much better idea where she was hiding him if the Director hadn't insisted I wear a hood before going on assignment. Just awful.
It's hard to breathe in those things, huh?
It is nice having someone here to talk to again, you, specifically.
Oh, well, of course, Fitz, but he has been rather distracted lately.
AIDA. You know how boys can be with their robots.
Speaking of the Director, here comes his little Smithers now. What do you want, Burrows?
The Director would like a word with Quake.
Well, not according to the press release. Now, if you'd follow me, ma'am.
